clues.clue: This 'show about nothing' ended in 1998 with its four leads sentenced to jail.
This 1983 finale, 'Goodbye, Farewell and Amen,' was long the most-watched U.S. broadcast.
This mob drama ended in 2007 with an abrupt cut to black in a diner.
The 2013 finale 'Felina' concluded this drama about a chemistry teacher named Walter White.
This 2010 finale revealed a 'flash-sideways' afterlife, dividing fans of a castaway drama.
This Menlo Park inventor held over 1,000 U.S. patents, including for the phonograph.
This Serbian-American engineer's alternating-current designs power the modern grid.
This British mathematician's 1936 'universal machine' helped found computer science.
This chemist invented dynamite and funded a famous set of annual prizes.
This physicist co-invented the transistor at Bell Labs and later promoted eugenics.
In April 1961 this Soviet cosmonaut became the first human in space.
Launched in 1957, this Soviet craft was the first artificial satellite.
In 1983 this American became the first U.S. woman in space.
In 1963 this Soviet became the first woman in space, surname Tereshkova.
Launched in 1977, this probe became the first human craft to enter interstellar space.
The Chicago Bulls wear this fiery color as their primary hue.
The Dallas Cowboys' helmet displays this five-pointed shape.
The Steelers' 'Steelmark' logo carries this number of diamond-like hypocycloids.
The Green Bay Packers' 'G' logo pairs green with this metallic color.
The Montreal Canadiens' interlocking logo combines a 'C' and this letter, for 'club de hockey.'
This furry green Philadelphia baseball mascot debuted in 1978.
The San Diego Padres' original mascot was this robed, smiling religious figure.
The University of Texas parades a live longhorn steer by this name.
The Phoenix Suns' purple costumed mascot is this dunking ape.
Notre Dame's pugnacious costumed mascot is this little figure with raised fists.

final.clue: In 'The Silence of the Lambs,' this villain coolly recalls eating a census taker's liver with fava beans.
